steps.title: Treat the repeated group as the target
steps.content: The question asks for $3-5x$, so do not expand it. Let the repeated group stay together while you move terms.

steps.title: Isolate the group
steps.content: Start with $$8-6(3-5x)=21-7(3-5x).$$ Subtract $8$ from both sides: $$-6(3-5x)=13-7(3-5x).$$ Add $7(3-5x)$ to both sides to get $$3-5x=13.$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The target expression has value 13. This is faster than solving for $x$ because the problem asks for the grouped expression itself.
